figures.caption: Table 3. Inference scaling the attention window size after training with a fixed window size consistently improves results. Colors reflect the improvement relative to the baseline setting in each column, which trains and evaluates with the same window size.

Table 6. Comparison of point cloud segmentation backbones across hardware platforms. Peak memory and runtimes are averaged over the ScanNet evaluation split [7]. CPU refers to Intel Core i5-10400F. mIoU is reported with test-time augmentation. PTX uses BF16 on all hardware, except FP8 for applicable linear layers on the NVIDIA H100. For fair comparison, all applicable networks are upgraded to FlashAttention-3 [38] on the H100.

Table 4. Impact of our contributions on PointTransformerX’s accuracy, parameter count, average runtime and average peak allocated memory for ScanNet [7] in comparison to the baseline PointTransformer V3 [47] on an NVIDIA H100.

Table 5. Ablation of the FFN design, varying the expansion ratio r ∈ {1, 2, 4}, activation function (GeLU, SwiGLU [39], ReLU2 [51]), and the use of DenseNet (DN) [18] skip connections. ReLU2 improves performance consistently, while DN skip connections yield mixed results at added complexity.
